# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.2.5

This release repairs the end-to-end Telegram workflow around session creation, Plan decisions,
approvals, file retrieval, and recoverable live status.

## Highlights

- Makes `/new`, `/changemodel`, and `/planmode` model/effort interactions consistent: explicit
  Exit actions, deterministic two/three-column rows, terminal selection messages, and no
  width-changing padding or default suffixes.
- Reconciles Plan decisions from both Telegram and the Codex TUI. Exact TUI approvals update the
  original Telegram Plan article and retire its buttons; observed TUI No/Escape decisions remove
  every Telegram chunk for that Plan.
- Preserves command approval messages after accept/decline and presents the three decisions in a
  clear one-column layout instead of deleting the audit trail.
- Runs `/ask` and `/getfile` in isolated read-only utility turns using the configured
  `gpt-5.6-luna` / `medium` defaults, fixes ephemeral-thread file lookup, and shows progress while
  file search is running.
- Clears recoverable reconnect/error indicators after the underlying session recovers, including
  the dynamic status message and discussion topic state.
- Removes the retired monolithic `bot.py` implementation in favor of the active control and
  discussion controllers.

## Upgrade notes

- The state database migrates automatically to schema v8 and creates a private pre-migration
  SQLite backup.
- Existing Plan publications are backfilled with their rendered text and repaired on startup so
  stale terminal actions can be retired.
- Re-running the v0.2.5 installer preserves existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state,
  and installer-owned service configuration.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
